title: FIN 3610 Midterm Exam
description: Covers Units 1-3: Foundations, Time Value of Money, and Valuing Projects & Firms. Closed book. 60 minutes. Single attempt.

questions.prompt: Which feature of the corporate form is most directly responsible for the existence of a public stock market?
questions.explanation: Transferable shares enable secondary-market trading without dissolving the firm.

questions.prompt: A firm reports Revenue = $2,000M, Net Income = $200M, Total Assets = $1,500M, Equity = $500M. What is Return on Equity (ROE)? Answer as a decimal (e.g. 0.20 for 20%).
Two assets produce identical cash flows in every state. The Law of One Price says:
choices: They must have identical risk premiums
They must trade in the same exchange
Their prices must be set by the same dealer
questions.explanation: Identical cash flows → identical prices, enforced by arbitrage.

questions.prompt: A project costs $1,000 today and pays $500 at the end of each of the next 3 years. Cost of capital is 10%. What is the NPV (in dollars, two decimals)?
You will receive $2,000 in 7 years. The discount rate is 5%. What is the present value? Answer to the nearest cent.
A perpetuity pays $400 per year forever starting next year, discount rate 5%. What is its PV?
A 5-year bond, face value $1,000, 6% annual coupon, YTM 5%. What is its price (to the nearest cent)?
Which of the following are legitimate uses of payback period?
choices: Quick liquidity screen for startups with short cash runway
Primary decision criterion for selecting between mutually exclusive 30-year projects
Definitive ranking when NPV and IRR disagree
questions.explanation: Payback is a screen and a communication tool; never the primary rule. It ignores TVM and tail cash flows.

questions.prompt: Project A: NPV = $100, IRR = 30%. Project B: NPV = $1,000, IRR = 15%. Cost of capital is 10%. Mutually exclusive. Which do you pick?
choices: B — higher dollar value created (NPV is the right criterion)
questions.explanation: IRR can favor smaller projects. NPV correctly ranks by dollar value created.

questions.prompt: A stock pays $3 in dividends next year, growing 5% forever. Cost of equity 11%. Gordon DDM value per share?
